key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share
key_assumptions.source: FY2025 cash from operations $704.8M minus immaterial capital spending, divided by ~78M diluted shares (pre-full Sandstorm weighting); Q1 2026 run-rate ~$8.3/sh on ~85M shares
Combined portfolio growth near inflation plus modest development ramp; exit ~22x cash flow

open_questions: Pending VIC PDF not in base IRR.
Sandstorm deferred tax and development conversion timing remain widest judgment bands.
Live price and final base IRR after mechanical refresh.
thesis: Royal Gold is a precious-metals royalty and streaming company headquartered in Denver. **[Fact]** It acquires and holds royalties, streams, and similar interests that entitle it to a share of production or revenue from mines operated by third parties, without Royal Gold acting as the mine operator. **[Fact]** Shares trade on Nasdaq (RGLD). **[Fact]** FY2025 revenue was **$1,030 million**, operating income **$638 million**, and net income **$466 million** (diluted earnings **$6.69** per share) per `investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20260219_rpt20251231_acc0000085535_26_000008.htm`. **[Fact]** FY2025 cash from operations was **$705 million**; the balance sheet expanded sharply after the Sandstorm Gold combination (goodwill and deferred taxes rose; total assets **$9.54 billion** at year-end 2025 versus **$3.39 billion** at year-end 2024).
why_market_wrong: The stock may trade as levered gold beta at a full multiple after the Sandstorm combination, while the underlying model is a high-margin toll on ounces with limited sustaining capital spending. **[Inference]** FY2025 earnings power benefited from both metal prices and enlarged attributable production; the market can treat peak-year cash flow as permanent. **[Inference]** Goodwill and deferred tax liabilities from purchase accounting make book value a poor anchor, but the economic asset is the contracted cash stream portfolio, not GAAP intangibles. **[Inference]** Proof-first component math at base case sums to about **$152** per share of economic value versus a price near **$217**, suggesting the market embeds development optionality and mid-cycle gold assumptions not yet reproduced in filing-locked owner-cash paths.
